tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Initial overview and binary selection

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()

steps.name: initial-overview
steps.review_criteria: As an RE professional who builds agentic analysis pipelines: Does this tool's initial state communicate what binaries are available and how to start analysis? Is the information hierarchy clear? Does it feel like a professional tool or a toy? Could this integrate into an automated analysis workflow?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Binary selection and loading

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

steps.name: binary-selection
steps.review_criteria: As someone who analyzes malware samples in batches: Is the binary selection workflow efficient? Can you switch between binaries quickly? Is there metadata shown before loading (file size, architecture, format)? Could you script binary loading for batch analysis? Is there a recent/favorites list?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Information density assessment

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const items = document.querySelectorAll('div, span'); for (const el of items) { if (el.textContent === 'droid' && el.offsetWidth > 0) { el.click(); return 'clicked'; } } return 'not found'; })()

steps.name: info-density
steps.review_criteria: As someone who works with information-dense RE tools: Is the screen real estate used efficiently? Are there too many empty areas or is it too cramped? Compared to IDA, Ghidra, or Binary Ninja, how does the information density compare? Would you want more data visible simultaneously or is it already overwhelming?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Load time and responsiveness

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

steps.name: load-time
steps.review_criteria: As someone who values fast iteration in analysis: How long did the initial data load take? Was there a loading indicator? Is the progressive loading strategy (seed then expand) acceptable or would you prefer full preload? For a malware triage workflow where you need results in seconds, is this fast enough?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Error state handling

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()

steps.name: error-states
steps.review_criteria: As someone who builds robust pipelines: What happens if the backend is down? Is there a clear error message? Does the UI degrade gracefully or crash? Are API errors surfaced to the user? Would you be able to diagnose connection issues from the UI alone? Is there a retry mechanism?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Graph layout quality for binary analysis

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const items = document.querySelectorAll('div, span'); for (const el of items) { if (el.textContent === 'droid' && el.offsetWidth > 0) { el.click(); return 'clicked'; } } return 'not found'; })()

steps.name: graph-layout
steps.review_criteria: As someone who builds graph-based analysis tools: Is the force-directed layout appropriate for binary call/data flow graphs? Are edge crossings minimized? Is the layout stable (nodes don't jump around)? Would a hierarchical layout be better for showing call hierarchies? Can you switch between layout algorithms?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Edge rendering and label clarity

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const items = document.querySelectorAll('div, span'); for (const el of items) { if (el.textContent === 'droid' && el.offsetWidth > 0) { el.click(); return 'clicked'; } } return 'not found'; })()

steps.name: edge-rendering
steps.review_criteria: As someone who analyzes data flow graphs: Are edges clearly rendered with visible arrowheads? Are edge labels readable at the current zoom level? Can you hover over an edge to see its properties? Are different edge types (call, reference, data flow) visually distinct? Is edge bundling used for nodes with many connections?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Node selection and detail loading

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const nodes = document.querySelectorAll('circle, [data-node-id]'); if (nodes.length > 0) { nodes[0].dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', { bubbles: true })); return 'clicked node'; } return 'no nodes'; })()

steps.name: node-selection
steps.review_criteria: As someone who clicks through hundreds of nodes during analysis: Is node selection responsive? Does the detail panel update immediately? Is there visual feedback for the selected node (highlight, border)? Can you see which node is selected in both graph and detail views? Is the selection state preserved when you switch views (graph/tree/table)?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Multi-select for batch operations

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const nodes = document.querySelectorAll('circle, [data-node-id]'); if (nodes.length > 1) { nodes[0].dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', { bubbles: true, ctrlKey: true })); nodes[1].dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', { bubbles: true, ctrlKey: true })); return 'multi-selected'; } return 'insufficient nodes'; })()

steps.name: multi-select
steps.review_criteria: As someone who needs batch operations on groups of functions: Can you select multiple nodes (Ctrl+click, lasso)? Are batch operations available (tag, annotate, export)? Is the multi-selection visually clear? Can you select by type/namespace/pattern? Would multi-select enable the batch analysis workflows you need for malware campaigns?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Expand neighbors interaction

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const nodes = document.querySelectorAll('circle, [data-node-id]'); if (nodes.length > 0) { nodes[0].dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('dblclick', { bubbles: true })); return 'double-clicked to expand'; } return 'no nodes'; })()

steps.name: expand-neighbors
steps.review_criteria: As someone who explores binary structure iteratively: Can you expand a node to show its neighbors? Is the expansion animated smoothly? Can you control expansion depth? Does expanding respect the progressive disclosure model? Can you expand only specific edge types (show callers but not callees)? Is there an undo for accidental expansion?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Collapse and simplify graph

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const items = document.querySelectorAll('div, span'); for (const el of items) { if (el.textContent === 'droid' && el.offsetWidth > 0) { el.click(); return 'clicked'; } } return 'not found'; })()

steps.name: collapse-graph
steps.review_criteria: As someone who needs to manage graph complexity: Can you collapse expanded nodes back? Can you collapse an entire namespace into a single node? Is there a way to remove nodes from the view without deleting them from the analysis? Can you undo/redo graph manipulation steps? Is the collapse animation smooth?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Zoom to fit and centering

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const items = document.querySelectorAll('div, span'); for (const el of items) { if (el.textContent === 'droid' && el.offsetWidth > 0) { el.click(); return 'clicked'; } } return 'not found'; })()

steps.name: zoom-to-fit
steps.review_criteria: As someone who frequently loses context in large graphs: Is there a zoom-to-fit button that shows all visible nodes? Does it maintain reasonable padding? Can you zoom to selection (show only selected nodes)? Does the auto-layout after expansion look sensible? Can you lock the current view to prevent automatic re-centering?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Breadcrumb and path navigation

steps.name: breadcrumb-nav
steps.review_criteria: As someone who traces deep call chains: Is there a breadcrumb trail showing your navigation path? Can you click breadcrumbs to jump back? Is the navigation history preserved? Can you see the full path from root to current node? Would you want a 'navigation sidebar' showing your analysis path like a browser history?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: RPC endpoint detail view

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const nodes = document.querySelectorAll('circle, [data-node-id]'); if (nodes.length > 0) { nodes[0].dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', { bubbles: true })); return 'clicked node'; } return 'no nodes'; })()

steps.name: rpc-detail
steps.review_criteria: As someone who analyzes IPC and RPC interfaces: When viewing an RPC endpoint or API node, are parameters shown with types? Are request/response schemas visible? Is the calling convention documented? Can you see which functions implement the endpoint? Are authentication/authorization requirements noted? Would this help you build a fuzzer for the interface?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: CLI command detail view

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const nodes = document.querySelectorAll('circle, [data-node-id]'); if (nodes.length > 1) { nodes[1].dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', { bubbles: true })); return 'clicked node'; } return 'insufficient nodes'; })()

steps.name: cli-detail
steps.review_criteria: As someone who scripts binary analysis: When viewing a CLI command or function, are arguments clearly listed? Are flag descriptions shown? Can you see the command's execution flow? Are environment variable dependencies noted? Is there enough information to write an automation script for this command?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Event and signal detail view

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const nodes = document.querySelectorAll('circle, [data-node-id]'); if (nodes.length > 2) { nodes[2].dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', { bubbles: true })); return 'clicked node'; } return 'insufficient nodes'; })()

steps.name: event-detail
steps.review_criteria: As someone who traces event-driven architectures in malware: When viewing an event or signal node, are the emitters and listeners shown? Is the event payload schema visible? Can you trace the event propagation path? Are timing constraints or ordering dependencies noted? Would this help you understand a malware C2 protocol?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Parameter and argument detail

steps.name: parameter-detail
steps.review_criteria: As someone who analyzes function signatures for exploitation: Are function parameters displayed with types, names, and calling convention? Are register assignments shown for ABI compliance? Can you see default values or constraints? Are variadic parameters indicated? Would this information be sufficient for writing a correct function call in an exploit?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Namespace and module detail

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const items = document.querySelectorAll('div, span'); for (const el of items) { if (el.textContent === 'droid' && el.offsetWidth > 0) { el.click(); return 'clicked'; } } return 'not found'; })()

steps.name: namespace-detail
steps.review_criteria: As someone who maps binary modules for analysis triage: When viewing a namespace node, can you see member count, exported symbols, and internal dependencies? Is the namespace's purpose summarized? Can you see which other namespaces it depends on? Would this help you identify the most interesting module in a large binary?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Schema and type definition detail

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const nodes = document.querySelectorAll('circle, [data-node-id]'); if (nodes.length > 2) { nodes[2].dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', { bubbles: true })); return 'clicked node'; } return 'insufficient nodes'; })()

steps.name: schema-detail
steps.review_criteria: As someone who reconstructs data structures from binaries: When viewing a schema or type node, are fields listed with types and offsets? Is the total size shown? Are nested types expandable? Is the struct layout consistent with the binary's architecture (32/64-bit, alignment)? Would you be able to write a ctypes definition from this view?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Tree hierarchy view

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.title === 'Tree view') { b.click(); return 'switched to tree'; } } return 'button not found'; })()

(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Tree') || b.title?.includes('tree')) { b.click(); return 'tree'; } } return 'not found'; })()

steps.name: tree-hierarchy
steps.review_criteria: As someone who navigates symbol tables: Is the tree hierarchy logical (binary → sections → namespaces → functions)? Are node types indicated by icons? Is the tree sortable (alphabetical, by address, by size)? Can you see the total count at each level? Does the tree mirror the actual binary structure or is it an abstraction?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Search within tree

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.title === 'Tree view') { b.click(); return 'switched to tree'; } } return 'button not found'; })()

(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Tree') || b.title?.includes('tree')) { b.click(); return 'tree'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const inputs = document.querySelectorAll('input[type=text], input[type=search], input:not([type])'); for (const i of inputs) { i.focus(); i.value = 'init'; i.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true })); return 'searched tree'; } return 'no input'; })()

steps.name: tree-search
steps.review_criteria: As someone who searches for specific symbols: Does the tree filter/search work with partial matches? Are matching nodes highlighted while non-matches are dimmed or hidden? Does it search across all levels of the hierarchy? Can you search by address or pattern? Is the search incremental (updates as you type)?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Expand and collapse tree nodes

steps.name: tree-expand-collapse
steps.review_criteria: As someone who navigates deep hierarchies: Can you expand/collapse individual tree branches? Is there expand-all/collapse-all? Does expanding a large branch cause a visible delay? Is the expand state preserved when you navigate away and return? Can you right-click to expand a specific branch level?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Tree context menu actions

steps.name: tree-context-menu
steps.review_criteria: As someone who needs quick actions from the tree: Does right-clicking a tree node offer context actions (show in graph, copy name, go to address, add to watchlist)? Are the actions relevant to the node type? Can you trigger analysis on a specific node from the tree? Is there a way to compare two tree nodes?

steps.name: show-in-graph
steps.review_criteria: As someone who switches between tree and graph views: Can you click a tree node to highlight it in the graph? Does the graph auto-center on the selected node? Is the transition smooth? Can you go back to the tree view with the same node selected? Is there bidirectional linking (clicking graph node highlights tree node)?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Copy symbol name from tree

steps.name: copy-symbol
steps.review_criteria: As someone who copies symbol names into scripts constantly: Can you easily copy a symbol's full qualified name? Is there a one-click copy button? Does it copy the mangled or demangled name? Can you copy the address alongside the name? Is the copied format suitable for pasting into a Python/IDAPython script?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Table columns and data completeness

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.title === 'Vulnerability / CWE mapping') { b.click(); return 'switched to vulns'; } } return 'button not found'; })()

(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Table') || b.title?.includes('table')) { b.click(); return 'table'; } } return 'not found'; })()

steps.name: table-columns
steps.review_criteria: As someone who analyzes function tables in binaries: Are the table columns appropriate (name, type, address, size, namespace, tags)? Can you customize which columns are visible? Are the column widths appropriate for the data? Is there a way to add custom columns for annotation? Would you want to export this table to CSV for batch processing?

tests.label: Tim Blazytko: Table sorting functionality

steps.script: (() => { const btn = document.querySelector('[data-testid="tab-final-site"]'); if (btn) { btn.click(); return 'clicked'; } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Seed Namespaces')) { b.click(); return 'seeded'; } } return 'not found'; })()

(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.title === 'Vulnerability / CWE mapping') { b.click(); return 'switched to vulns'; } } return 'button not found'; })()

(() => { const btns = document.querySelectorAll('button'); for (const b of btns) { if (b.textContent?.includes('Table') || b.title?.includes('table')) { b.click(); return 'table'; } } return 'not found'; })()
(() => { const headers = document.querySelectorAll('th, [role=columnheader]'); if (headers.length > 0) { headers[0].click(); return 'sorted by ' + headers[0].textContent; } return 'no headers'; })()

steps.name: table-sorting
steps.review_criteria: As someone who sorts by complexity or size to find interesting targets: Can you sort by clicking column headers? Is the sort direction indicated (arrow)? Can you multi-column sort? Is sorting stable? Can you sort by numeric values correctly (not lexicographic)? Would you want custom sort expressions?

steps.name: cwe-column
steps.review_criteria: As someone who maps binary patterns to CWE weaknesses: Is there a CWE column in the table? Are CWE IDs displayed as clickable links? Can you filter to show only entries with specific CWEs? Are the CWE mappings sourced from static analysis or LLM inference? Is the confidence level shown? Would this help you triage vulnerability candidates?

steps.name: security-perspective
steps.review_criteria: As someone who focuses on security analysis: Is there a security-focused view that highlights attack surfaces, vulnerable functions, and security-relevant code paths? Does the security perspective filter out irrelevant nodes? Are risk scores or severity indicators shown? Would this accelerate a security audit compared to generic code browsing?

steps.name: data-flow-perspective
steps.review_criteria: As someone who traces data flow for taint analysis: Is there a data flow perspective showing how data moves through the binary? Can you trace from input source to processing sink? Are data transformations visible on edges? Would this replace or complement a dedicated taint analysis tool? Can you define custom sources and sinks?

steps.name: all-perspective
steps.review_criteria: As someone who needs the full picture: Does the 'all entities' view show everything without filtering? Is it still navigable or does it become overwhelming? Are there statistics (node count, edge count, type distribution)? Would you use this for initial triage before switching to a focused perspective? Is the overview useful for identifying the binary's main components?

steps.name: perspective-filtering
steps.review_criteria: As someone who switches between analysis modes frequently: Can you switch perspectives without losing context? Is the switching instant or does it reload data? Can you create custom perspectives (e.g., 'crypto functions + their callers')? Are perspectives named and saveable? Would you define new perspectives based on your analysis needs?

steps.name: save-scene
steps.review_criteria: As someone who works on analysis across multiple sessions: Can you save the current scene (graph state, selections, annotations)? Is there a save/load UI? Is the scene format portable (could you share it with a colleague)? Does the scene include chat history? Would scene management enable reproducible analysis workflows?

steps.name: load-scene
steps.review_criteria: As someone who juggles multiple analysis projects: Can you load a previously saved scene? Is the loading fast? Does it restore all state (graph position, zoom, selected node, active filters)? Can you maintain multiple named scenes and switch between them? Is there an auto-save feature?

steps.name: clear-scene
steps.review_criteria: As someone who needs clean starts for each analysis: Is there a clear/reset function? Does it confirm before clearing (to prevent accidental loss)? Does clearing preserve saved scenes? Can you clear just the graph while keeping settings? Is the empty state after clearing the same as the initial state?

steps.name: scene-comparison
steps.review_criteria: As someone who compares binary versions: Can you compare two scenes (e.g., two versions of the same binary)? Is there a diff view showing added/removed/changed nodes? Can you overlay two graphs? Would scene comparison support patch analysis or malware variant comparison? Is the comparison automated or manual?

steps.name: journal-recording
steps.review_criteria: As someone who documents analysis for reproducibility: Is there an investigation journal that records your actions? Are navigation steps, queries, and findings logged automatically? Can you add manual notes? Is the journal timestamped? Would this journal be useful for writing a technical report or training an agentic analysis pipeline?

steps.name: step-tracking
steps.review_criteria: As someone who builds reproducible analysis workflows: Can you replay recorded analysis steps? Is the step sequence exportable as a script? Could you use recorded steps to train an agentic RE system? Is there a way to branch the analysis history (explore alternative hypotheses)? Would step tracking enable 'analysis recipes' that others could follow?

steps.name: export-report
steps.review_criteria: As someone who delivers analysis reports: Can you export the investigation journal as a formatted report (Markdown, PDF, HTML)? Does the report include graph screenshots, code snippets, and findings? Are CWE/ATT&CK references included? Is the report template customizable? Would this replace your manual report-writing workflow?

steps.name: session-continuity
steps.review_criteria: As someone who works on analysis over days: Does the tool preserve state across browser reloads? Is the session stored locally or server-side? Can you resume from where you left off? Is there auto-save? What happens if the browser crashes — is there recovery? Would session continuity make this viable for multi-day malware analysis campaigns?
